title: Tahdig (Crispy Saffron Rice)
description: The prized golden, crispy crust that forms at the bottom of the pot of Persian rice. Infused with saffron and butter, it's the ultimate crunchy delicacy.

ingredients.note: Washed until water runs clear, then soaked in salted water for 2 hours
Ground, bloomed in 2 tbsp hot water
For the bottom of the pot
Full fat, for the crust base
Melted, to pour over steaming rice

instructions.text: Bring 8 cups of water and 3 tbsp of salt to a rolling boil in a large non-stick pot.

Drain the soaked rice and add it to the boiling water. Boil uncovered for 5-8 minutes. The rice grains should be slightly soft on the outside but firm in the middle. Drain the rice in a fine-mesh colander and rinse lightly with lukewarm water.
In a medium bowl, mix the yogurt, bloomed saffron, and 1 cup of the parboiled drained rice until evenly coated.

Wash and dry the non-stick pot. Place it over medium heat and add the oil/ghee. Once the oil is hot, spread the yogurt-saffron-rice mixture evenly across the bottom of the pot, packing it down gently with a spatula to form a flat layer.

Gently spoon the remaining parboiled white rice over the tahdig layer, shaping it into a loose pyramid or mound. Using the handle of a wooden spoon, poke 4-5 holes straight down through the rice to the bottom to allow steam to escape.
Pour the melted butter and 2 tablespoons of water over the rice pyramid.
Wrap the pot lid in a clean kitchen towel (to catch condensation) and place it tightly on the pot. Cook on medium-high heat for 5-7 minutes until steam builds up inside the pot.
Reduce the heat to the lowest setting and let it steam undisturbed for 45-50 minutes. Do not open the lid during this time.
To serve, remove the lid, place a large round serving platter upside down over the pot, and carefully but quickly invert the pot to release the rice cake. The crispy golden tahdig should be on top.

tips: Using a good quality non-stick pot (like Teflon) is essential for a perfect release. If the tahdig won't release easily, dip the bottom of the hot pot into a sink of cold water for 1 minute before inverting.
storage_notes: Tahdig is best eaten immediately while crispy. Leftovers can be stored in the fridge for 2 days but will lose their crunch. Reheat in a toaster oven.

substitutions.note: Instead of the yogurt-rice mix, you can layer thinly sliced potatoes or flatbread at the bottom of the oiled pot.
